description: CNI plugin with integrated IPAM for Kubernetes/containerd

stories.title: IPAM: release IP allocation on DEL
stories.description: Add ReleaseAddr(containerID string) error to pkg/ipam/ipam.go that removes the matching entry from allocations.json. Wire it into network.TeardownNetwork so DEL actually frees the IP. Currently TeardownNetwork only deletes the host veth, leaving a stale allocation in the store.
stories.acceptance: go test ./pkg/ipam/... passes; calling BindNewAddr then ReleaseAddr then BindNewAddr again reuses the same IP.

stories.title: Add default route inside container netns
stories.description: In network.SetupNetwork, after BindNewAddr succeeds inside the netns.Do block, call netlink.RouteAdd to install a default route via the gateway IP from ipamConfig.Ranges[0][0].Gateway. If no gateway is configured, skip. Use the netlinkwrapper interface (n.netlink) so it is mockable.
stories.acceptance: make vet && make build pass; manual test shows 'ip route' inside container netns has a default route.

stories.title: Expand iptableswrapper and add masquerade rule
stories.description: Uncomment and implement the Append/AppendUnique/Exists/Delete methods on IPTablesIface in pkg/iptableswrapper/iptables.go. Then in network.SetupNetwork, after veth setup, call AppendUnique on the POSTROUTING chain in the nat table with a MASQUERADE rule for the pod subnet. Add a corresponding delete in TeardownNetwork only if the bridge has no remaining ports.
stories.acceptance: make vet && make build pass; iptableswrapper unit test covers Append/Exists/Delete via a mock.

stories.title: Unit tests for pkg/ipam
stories.description: Write pkg/ipam/ipam_test.go with table-driven tests. Set IPAMConfig.DataDir to t.TempDir() to avoid filesystem side effects. Cover: (1) BindNewAddr allocates an IP in range, (2) two allocations get different IPs, (3) ReleaseAddr frees the IP and a third BindNewAddr reuses it, (4) ReleaseStaleAllocations removes only invalid container IDs, (5) CheckStatus returns error when no ranges configured.
stories.acceptance: go test ./pkg/ipam/... -v passes without root privileges.

stories.title: Unit tests for pkg/network with mock interfaces
stories.description: Add mock implementations of netlinkwrapper.NetLink and nswrapper.NS (using simple structs that record calls and return preset values). Write pkg/network/network_test.go covering: (1) SetupNetwork happy path returns an addr, (2) SetupNetwork rolls back veth if bridge attach fails, (3) TeardownNetwork calls LinkDel with correct name, (4) CheckNetwork returns error when host veth missing.
stories.acceptance: go test ./pkg/network/... -v passes without root privileges.

stories.title: Integration test shell script via cnitool
stories.description: Create scripts/integration-test.sh. It should: build the binary with 'make build', install to /opt/cni/bin, create a test CNI config at /etc/cni/net.d/99-eureka-test.conflist, create a network namespace, invoke the plugin directly with CNI_COMMAND=ADD/CHECK/DEL env vars and validate the JSON result, then clean up. Requires root; skip gracefully if not root.
stories.acceptance: scripts/integration-test.sh exits 0 on a machine with the build dependencies installed.

stories.title: Multi-stage Dockerfile for CNI installer image
stories.description: Create a Dockerfile at the repo root using a two-stage build. Stage 1 (builder): golang:1.22-alpine, copy source, run 'go build -o probable-eureka .'. Stage 2 (installer): use a minimal base (e.g. alpine:3.19), copy only the binary. The image's default CMD should be 'cp /probable-eureka /opt/cni/bin/probable-eureka' so it can be used as a DaemonSet init container. Add a 'make image' target to the Makefile that runs 'docker build -t probable-eureka:latest .'.
stories.acceptance: make image exits 0; docker run --rm -v /tmp/cni-test:/opt/cni/bin probable-eureka:latest && ls /tmp/cni-test/probable-eureka succeeds.

stories.title: Production CNI conflist template
stories.description: Create deployments/10-eureka.conflist with a valid CNI 1.0.0 conflist that references the probable-eureka plugin. Include sensible production defaults: name 'eureka', bridge 'cni0', dataDir '/var/lib/cni/eureka', subnet '10.244.0.0/16', gateway '10.244.0.1', mtu 1500. Add comments (via a companion deployments/10-eureka.conflist.example) explaining each field. The conflist must pass 'jq . deployments/10-eureka.conflist' (valid JSON).
stories.acceptance: jq . deployments/10-eureka.conflist exits 0; the plugin name field matches the binary name 'probable-eureka'.

stories.title: Kubernetes DaemonSet manifest for node installation
stories.description: Create deployments/daemonset.yaml. The DaemonSet should: (1) run on every node via a nodeSelector or tolerations for all taints, (2) use an initContainer with image probable-eureka:latest that copies the binary to /opt/cni/bin via a hostPath volume mount, (3) use a second initContainer (busybox or alpine) that writes the CNI conflist from a ConfigMap to /etc/cni/net.d/10-eureka.conflist, (4) include a pause main container so the Pod stays Running after init. Create deployments/configmap.yaml holding the conflist content from deploy-conflist. Both manifests must pass 'kubectl apply --dry-run=client -f deployments/'.
stories.acceptance: kubectl apply --dry-run=client -f deployments/ exits 0 against any reachable cluster or with --validate=false on a machine without a cluster.

stories.title: Add install and uninstall Makefile targets for direct node deployment
stories.description: Add the following targets to the Makefile: (1) 'install': requires root, copies the built binary to /opt/cni/bin/probable-eureka (mode 0755), copies deployments/10-eureka.conflist to /etc/cni/net.d/10-eureka.conflist; (2) 'uninstall': removes both files; (3) update the existing 'help' target (or add one) that lists all targets with one-line descriptions. Guard install/uninstall with a root check: '[ $$(id -u) -eq 0 ] || (echo "install requires root"; exit 1)'.
stories.acceptance: make install (run as root) places files at the correct paths; make uninstall removes them; make help lists install and uninstall.
